instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on What is Claim Settlement Process For ULIPs? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: claim settlement process, ulip insurance, unit-linked insurance
Body Content:
A unit-linked insurance plan can give your loved ones the safety cover they deserve. While the maturity benefits from such plans can be enjoyed when you survive the policy term, the death benefit payout from a unit-linked insurance policy can ensure the welfare of your dependents after your demise. It can help them achieve their life goals, even in your absence.
However, before you buy a ULIP, it is important to know about the claim settlement process of such plans. Here’s an overview of how the settlement process works:
Intimating the Insurance Company
If you happen to pass away during the policy tenure, your listed beneficiary has to intimate the insurance company of the same. Since notifying the insurance company is the preliminary step in the ULIP claim settlement process, the sooner this step is completed, the faster you can progress with the following steps in this process.
Filing the Claim Form
Once the company has been notified, your nominee will have to fill in a claim form. Usually, ULIP insurance claim forms can be downloaded from the insurer’s website. This form will require information about certain key details like the policy number, name of the policyholder, date of death as well as some basic details about the claimant. Your nominee will also have to present a set of documents when filing the claim.
Documents Needed
Generally, your nominee will need to furnish the following set of documents for a unit-linked insurance claim:
The policy document in original
Copies of the death certificate
Identification and address proof of the claimant
However, in the event of accidental death, nominees must also submit a copy of the first-information report (FIR) and autopsy report to process such ULIP insurance claims. If the death has happened due to a critical illness, they will also need to submit hospital reports and other medical records.
The Claim Settlement Process
To rule out foul play and ensure that the policy payout reaches the right individuals, the insurance company will start reviewing the filed claim. If any inconsistencies are noted, the company can ask for clarifications within 15 days of the claim being filed.
As per the IRDAI guidelines, if no investigation is needed, insurers can clear claims within 30 days of receiving the form and the attached documents. However, for unit-linked insurance claims that do need investigating, the time cap is placed at 180 days.
Points to Remember
Carefully Assess the Information You Are Providing
While most unit-linked insurance plans have a high claim settlement ratio, your nominee’s claim may be rejected due to misinformation or non-disclosure of facts. If there’s a mismatch between the information provided on the claim form and your policy document, for instance, the spelling of your name or your date of birth, your nominee may not receive the death benefit payout from the policy. Carefully reviewing insurance forms will help maintain consistency and avoid cardinal errors.
Remain Truthful About Your Lifestyle Habits and Medical Conditions
Similarly, non-disclosures of a medical condition or habits like smoking can lead to claim rejections. Since insurance companies calculate your risk based on the assessment of such factors, withholding this information can cost you during the claim settlement process.
Payout Timelines Can Vary
While the IRDAI mandates that the claim settlement process has to be completed within 30 days (for dispute-free claims) and 180 days (for those needing investigations), insurance companies also have their own timelines in place. Generally, these timelines fall well within the upper limit mandated by the IRDAI. Nonetheless, the bottom line remains that you have to be patient when waiting for your claim to get processed.
E-Payments
Once all the documents are reviewed and the claim is processed, your beneficiary will receive the death benefit payout. This payout cannot be availed in cash as insurers are bound to release it only through an electronic payment mode.
Summing It Up
With the right documents in place, the ULIP claim settlement process becomes quite easy and hassle-free. Moreover, your nominee can also seek assistance from the insurance company at any stage of this process to further simplify it. Once the claim is settled the payout can be used to fund your child’s education or meet the welfare needs of your spouse.
